explanation: Watt, Ohm, Volt, and Ampere are all SI units specifically used to measure electrical quantities (power, resistance, voltage, current respectively). Joule is the SI unit for energy, a broader physical quantity not exclusively electrical.
Plato, Aristotle, Socrates, and Epicurus were influential ancient Greek philosophers. Caesar, while a significant historical figure, was a Roman general and statesman, not primarily a philosopher.
Cereal (serial), Cell (sell), Sense (scents), and Scene (seen) each have a common homophone. Chair does not have a commonly recognized homophone.
